examples.sentence: 遅れる理由がないなら、遅れるべきではありません。
examples.translation: If there's no reason to be late, you shouldn’t be late.
examples.translationEn: If there's no reason to be late, you shouldn’t be late.
examples.romaji: Okureru riyuu ga nai nara, okureru beki dewa arimasen.

examples.sentence: 子供に悪口を言うべきではありません。
examples.translation: You shouldn’t say hurtful things to children.
examples.translationEn: You shouldn’t say hurtful things to children.
examples.romaji: Kodomo ni waruguchi o iu beki dewa arimasen.

examples.sentence: この薬は一度に飲みすぎるべきではない。
examples.translation: You should not take too much of this medicine at once.
examples.translationEn: You should not take too much of this medicine at once.
examples.romaji: Kono kusuri wa ichido ni nomisugiru beki dewa nai.

examples.sentence: 知らない人と個人情報を共有するべきではない。
examples.translation: You shouldn't share personal information with strangers.
examples.translationEn: You shouldn't share personal information with strangers.
examples.romaji: Shiranai hito to kojin jouhou o kyouyuu suru beki dewa nai.

examples.sentence: 彼は今晩来るまい。
examples.translation: He probably won't come tonight.
examples.translationEn: He probably won't come tonight.
examples.romaji: Kare wa konban kuru mai.

examples.sentence: こんなに遅れては、電車に間に合うまい。
examples.translation: Being this late, I probably won't make it for the train.
examples.translationEn: Being this late, I probably won't make it for the train.
examples.romaji: Konnani okurete wa, densha ni maniau mai.

examples.sentence: あの人は絶対に忘れるまい。
examples.translation: That person will definitely not forget.
examples.translationEn: That person will definitely not forget.
examples.romaji: Ano hito wa zettai ni wasureru mai.

examples.sentence: 私は二度とその失敗を繰り返すまい。
examples.translation: I will not repeat that mistake again.
examples.translationEn: I will not repeat that mistake again.
examples.romaji: Watashi wa nidoto sono shippai o kurikaesu mai.

examples.sentence: 9時まで働いて、その後休憩しました。
examples.translation: I worked until 9 o'clock, and then took a break.
examples.translationEn: I worked until 9 o'clock, and then took a break.
examples.romaji: Ku-ji made hataraite, sono ato kyuukei shimashita.

examples.sentence: 夜まで勉強して、試験に合格しました。
examples.translation: I studied until night, and passed the exam.
examples.translationEn: I studied until night, and passed the exam.
examples.romaji: Yoru made benkyou shite, shiken ni goukaku shimashita.

examples.sentence: 最後のページまで読んで、本を閉じました。
examples.translation: I read until the last page, then closed the book.
examples.translationEn: I read until the last page, then closed the book.
examples.romaji: Saigo no peeji made yonde, hon o tojimashita.

examples.sentence: 電車が着くまで待って、友達と会いました。
examples.translation: I waited until the train arrived, and then met my friend.
examples.translationEn: I waited until the train arrived, and then met my friend.
examples.romaji: Densha ga tsuku made matte, tomodachi to aimashita.

examples.sentence: 子供が寝ているままに、静かに部屋を出ました。
examples.translation: I quietly left the room while the child was still sleeping.
examples.translationEn: I quietly left the room while the child was still sleeping.
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga nete iru mama ni, shizuka ni heya o demashita.

examples.sentence: 窓が開いたままで、寒くなりました。
examples.translation: It got cold with the window left open.
examples.translationEn: It got cold with the window left open.
examples.romaji: Mado ga aita mama de, samuku narimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼は元気なままで老人ホームに入りました。
examples.translation: He entered the nursing home while still in good health.
examples.translationEn: He entered the nursing home while still in good health.
examples.romaji: Kare wa genki na mama de roujin hoomu ni hairimashita.

examples.sentence: 昔の思い出のままに、この町に戻りました。
examples.translation: I returned to this town with my old memories intact.
examples.translationEn: I returned to this town with my old memories intact.
examples.romaji: Mukashi no omoide no mama ni, kono machi ni modorimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼は周りの人もかまわず、大声で話しました。
examples.translation: Without worrying about the people around him, he spoke loudly.
examples.translationEn: Without worrying about the people around him, he spoke loudly.
examples.romaji: Kare wa mawari no hito mo kamawazu, oogoe de hanashimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼女は時間もかまわず、ゆっくりと本を読み続けました。
examples.translation: She kept reading the book leisurely, without worrying about the time.
examples.translationEn: She kept reading the book leisurely, without worrying about the time.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa jikan mo kamawazu, yukkuri to hon o yomitsuzukemashita.

examples.sentence: 彼は寒さもかまわず、外で運動しました。
examples.translation: He exercised outside without worrying about the cold.
examples.translationEn: He exercised outside without worrying about the cold.
examples.romaji: Kare wa samusa mo kamawazu, soto de undou shimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼女は危険もかまわず、火事の中に飛び込んで助けに行きました。
examples.translation: She jumped into the fire without fear of danger to help.
examples.translationEn: She jumped into the fire without fear of danger to help.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kiken mo kamawazu, kaji no naka ni tobikonde tasuke ni ikimashita.

examples.sentence: そんなこと、僕がやりたいものか。
examples.translation: No way do I want to do such a thing.
examples.translationEn: No way do I want to do such a thing.
examples.romaji: Sonna koto, boku ga yaritai mono ka.

examples.sentence: こんな仕事、誰が引き受けようものか。
examples.translation: Who on earth would take on a job like this? (No one would!)
examples.translationEn: Who on earth would take on a job like this? (No one would!)
examples.romaji: Konna shigoto, dare ga hikiukeyou mono ka.

examples.sentence: 誰が彼に助けを求めようものか。
examples.translation: Who would ever ask him for help? (Certainly no one!)
examples.translationEn: Who would ever ask him for help? (Certainly no one!)
examples.romaji: Dare ga kare ni tasuke o motomeyou mono ka.

examples.sentence: この寒い日に外で遊ぶなんて、無理なものか。
examples.translation: There's no way we can play outside on a day this cold.
examples.translationEn: There's no way we can play outside on a day this cold.
examples.romaji: Kono samui hi ni soto de asobu nante, muri na mono ka.

examples.sentence: 彼のスピーチには心を打たれるものがある。
examples.translation: There's something moving about his speech.
examples.translationEn: There's something moving about his speech.
examples.romaji: Kare no supiichi ni wa kokoro o utareru mono ga aru.

examples.sentence: この映画には考えさせられるものがある。
examples.translation: There's something about this movie that really makes you think.
examples.translationEn: There's something about this movie that really makes you think.
examples.romaji: Kono eiga ni wa kangaesaserareru mono ga aru.

examples.sentence: 彼の言うことにも一理あるものがある。
examples.translation: There's a certain point in what he says.
examples.translationEn: There's a certain point in what he says.
examples.romaji: Kare no iu koto ni mo ichiri aru mono ga aru.

examples.sentence: 毎日忙しすぎる生活には少し息苦しいものがある。
examples.translation: There's something a bit suffocating about being so busy every day.
examples.translationEn: There's something a bit suffocating about being so busy every day.
examples.romaji: Mainichi isogashisugiru seikatsu ni wa sukoshi ikigurushii mono ga aru.

examples.sentence: 若い時はたくさん遊ぶものだ。
examples.translation: It is natural to have a lot of fun when you are young.
examples.translationEn: It is natural to have a lot of fun when you are young.
examples.romaji: Wakai toki wa takusan asobu mono da.

examples.sentence: 約束をしたら、守るものだ。
examples.translation: If you make a promise, you should keep it.
examples.translationEn: If you make a promise, you should keep it.
examples.romaji: Yakusoku o shitara, mamoru mono da.

examples.sentence: お客さんが来ると、お茶を出すものだ。
examples.translation: When guests arrive, it's customary to serve tea.
examples.translationEn: When guests arrive, it's customary to serve tea.
examples.romaji: Okyakusan ga kuru to, ocha o dasu mono da.

examples.sentence: 人生は困難に立ち向かうものだ。
examples.translation: In life, one ought to face hardships.
examples.translationEn: In life, one ought to face hardships.
examples.romaji: Jinsei wa konnan ni tachimukau mono da.
